text: Running performance can be accurately modeled using two parameters: an aerobic power index and an endurance index that describes the decline in sustainable power over duration.
The endurance index, which captures how sustainable power declines over increasing duration, enables highly accurate race time prediction from training data.
The lactate threshold can be identified as a key parameter from the mathematical modeling of the endurance index derived from wearable tracker data.
Correlations exist between training volume/intensity and performance indices (aerobic power and endurance), pointing to an optimal balance of training load for performance improvement.
Real-world wearable data containing only duration and distance from training sessions is sufficient to derive meaningful performance parameters for race prediction.
